title: After-Action Report — rename "Savant / Statcast" → "O27 Index" (O27i)

rationale.item: The advanced-stats hub shipped under borrowed MLB branding — "Savant" for the percentile player page, "Statcast" for the batted-ball leaderboard. That reads as a knockoff and doesn't fit the house voice, which already has period-styled in-world publications (the Almanac and the Gazette). The hub was renamed to O27 Index (short form O27i) — named for the game itself, in keeping with that voice.

actions.description: Controlled, repo-wide token rename — no behaviour change, pure identity/plumbing: routes /savant → /o27i, /leaderboard/statcast → /o27i/leaders, /player/<id>/savant → /player/<id>/o27i; endpoints savant_home/statcast_leaderboard/player_savant → o27i_home/o27i_leaders/player_o27i; templates savant.html/savant_home.html/statcast_leaderboard.html → o27i_player.html/o27i_home.html/o27i_leaders.html; internal helpers _SAVANT_*/_savant_* → _O27I_*/_o27i_*; nav label and page titles updated; player-page button "Savant" → "O27i".
CSS classes (.savant-* → .o27i-*), search-box element ids, and the JS redirect path renamed so no savant/statcast token survives in any served page.

risk_posture: A controlled, repo-wide token rename — no behaviour change, pure identity/plumbing
No engine, schema, or stat-math change — this was branding + routing only.

validation_claims.claim: Every new route returns 200 (/o27i, /o27i/leaders, /player/<id>/o27i, and the JSON variant); the three old routes return 404.
validation_claims.check_method: Run the app and curl the new and old routes

validation_claims.claim: Nav reads "O27 Index" and links to /o27i; the player-page button reads "O27i" and links through.
Grep confirms zero savant / Savant / statcast / Statcast strings in any rendered page (home, leaders, player percentile page).
validation_claims.check_method: grep -ri savant\|statcast over o27v2/web and rendered output

non_actions.item: Historical AARs keep their original names and contents (aar-baseball-savant-feasibility.md, aar-savant-percentile-page.md, aar-savant-phases-2-4.md)
non_actions.reason: Those document how the page was built at the time and are an accurate record; renaming them would falsify history. They reference "Savant" because that's what it was called then.

non_actions.item: No engine, schema, or stat-math change

open_hooks: This rename was the first commit of a larger arc on the same branch (expanded metric suite, Prospect Index, exact OAA/WP) — see docs/aar-expanded-metrics-and-prospect-index.md.
section_headers_original: What I deliberately did NOT change
parser_notes: Short, clean rename AAR. non_actions category for keeping historical AARs is arguably agent-decided; recorded as declined_by_owner per the document's deliberate phrasing — could also be read as out_of_scope.
